title: [lora_l2.c:379-387] lora_modem_config.tx=true has no effect for half-duplex radios
description:
Category not checked: Hardware abstraction correctness

Bug found: At line 386, the LoRa config sets .tx = true. Looking at the comment at line 383:
  .tx_power = CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_TX_POWER,
  .tx = true,

The .tx field in struct lora_modem_config is documented in Zephyr as:
'Set to true for TX mode, false for RX mode. Some radios need this hint.'

For half-duplex LoRa radios (which is all of them - SX127x, SX126x), the radio is either in TX or RX mode, not both. Setting .tx = true during configuration doesn't enable simultaneous TX/RX; it just tells the radio 'prepare for TX'.

But immediately after lora_config(), we start the RX thread which calls lora_recv(). The radio will switch to RX mode for lora_recv(), making the .tx = true meaningless.

The .tx = true is harmless but misleading. Better would be to set .tx = false since we're primarily an RX listener, or omit it (defaults to false).

Impact: P4 - Misleading config, no functional impact.
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation explaining .tx=true is effectively unused since RX thread calls lora_recv() first, which switches to RX mode. Kept for driver default behavior and PA/LNA initialization hints.
